{{Quote|In 1993 I lucked into the first "Tales of the Jedi" story from Dark Horse Comics, stepping through the door into the world of publishing.|Janine Johnston|<ref name="About" />}}
'''Janine Johnston''' is a [[Wikipedia:Canada|Canadian]] artist who has worked on various different genres of comics and roleplaying games. Johnston is known among the ''[[Star Wars]]'' community for her artwork which appeared in [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']].

==Biography==
Born and raised in [[Wikipedia:Victoria, British Columbia|Victoria]], [[Wikipedia:British Columbia|British Columbia]] Janine Johnston starting selling her artwork at the age of fifteen. As she grew older, Johnston began doing freelance contract work for a large number of things, such as television props, compact disc covers, logos, and tattoos designs.

In [[1993]] she worked in conjunction with author [[Tom Veitch]], and penciled the first issue of [[Tales of the Jedi: The Saga of Nomi Sunrider|''The Saga of Nomi Sunrider'']], which is the second story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] line of [[Star Wars comic books|comics]]. Aside from her work on ''[[Star Wars]]'', Johnston has provided artwork for the comic series [[Wikipedia:Elfquest|''Elfquest'']], as well as a series of comics by author [[Wikipedia:Drew Hayes|Drew Hayes]]. Johnston has also done work for [[Wikipedia:Magic the Gathering|Magic the Gathering]], [[Wikipedia:Everway & Spherewalker|Everway & Spherewalker]], [[Wikipedia:Warhammer Fantasy Roleplay|Warhammer]], [[Wikipedia:Legend of the Five Rings|Legend of the Five Rings]], among various others.
